tldr: Replace Anteon with Qryn, an open-source polyglot observability backend on ClickHouse exposing Loki, Prometheus, Tempo, and Datadog-compatible APIs.
why_switch: Open-source (AGPL-3.0) with no per-GB ingest billing.
Single ClickHouse backend for logs, metrics, and traces.
API-compatible with Loki, Prometheus, Tempo, Datadog — minimizes client changes.
Strong cost efficiency via ClickHouse compression.
Avoid Anteon's pricing spikes on heavy ingest.
what_to_export: Historical telemetry you want to keep
export_method: Anteon lacks a single documented export bundle. Use its API or self-hosted DB to pull configuration; archive logs/metrics/traces to object storage if you need historical data. See Anteon's official docs for current export options.
import_method: Qryn deploys via Docker, Helm, or directly on a ClickHouse cluster (see qryn.dev docs). Point your existing OpenTelemetry, Loki, Prometheus remote_write, or Datadog-format clients at Qryn's compatible endpoints. Recreate dashboards in Grafana using Qryn's Loki/Prometheus/Tempo data sources. Rebuild alert rules in Alertmanager or Grafana alerting.

concept_mapping.note: Single store for logs, metrics, traces.
Existing OTel/Loki/Prom clients can usually re-point with config change.
Rebuild; do not assume direct portability.
Qryn is a backend; pair with Beyla/Cilium/eBPF agents if needed.
No ingest fees; you operate ClickHouse.

gotchas: Qryn is a backend, not a full collection agent — you still need OTel/Loki/Prom shippers.
ClickHouse operations expertise required (sizing, replication, retention).
API compatibility is high but not 100%; test critical queries early.
AGPL-3.0 — review license obligations if you plan to embed/modify it.
Plan a parallel-run window: dual-write or dual-collect during cutover.
Smaller managed-service ecosystem than Grafana Cloud.

email.subject: Migrating from Anteon to Qryn
email.body:
Hi team,

Looking to swap Anteon for Qryn. Qryn is an open-source ClickHouse-backed observability stack with Loki, Prometheus, Tempo, and Datadog-compatible APIs — meaning fewer client changes and no per-GB ingest fees.

Key work: stand up ClickHouse + Qryn; re-point existing collectors to Qryn endpoints; rebuild Grafana dashboards and Alertmanager rules; verify query parity for our top dashboards before cutover.

Caveats: AGPL-3.0 license to review, and we'll own ClickHouse ops.

Estimated effort: 16-40 hours plus a parallel-run window.

Will share a detailed plan and pilot tenant.

Thanks.
